sentence: La chichí preparó cochinita pibil para la comida.
ipa: ˈla tʃiˈtʃi pɾepaˈɾo kotʃiˈnita piˈbil ˈpaɾa ˈla koˈmida
gloss_en: Grandma made cochinita pibil for lunch.
notes: Yucatec lexicon 'chichí' (grandmother, Maya), 'cochinita pibil'; seseo baseline; occlusive intervocalic /b d g/ (no spirantisation) per michnowicz2012 and lope_blanch1987; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.

sentence: El chan niño se comió toda la marquesita.
ipa: ˈel ˈtʃam ˈniɲo ˈse koˈmio ˈtoda ˈla maɾkeˈsita
gloss_en: The little boy ate the whole marquesita.
notes: Maya adstrate diminutive 'chan' (little); 'marquesita' (Yucatec street snack); occlusive intervocalic /b d g/ (no spirantisation) per michnowicz2012 and lope_blanch1987; word-final /n/->[m] bilabialisation per michnowicz2008 and lope_blanch1987; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.

sentence: Ese huach no conoce el calor de Mérida.
ipa: ˈese ˈwatʃ ˈno koˈnose ˈel kaˈloɾ ˈde ˈmeɾida
gloss_en: That outsider doesn't know Mérida's heat.
notes: Yucatec 'huach/wach' (non-peninsular Mexican); seseo in 'ese/conoce'; occlusive intervocalic /b d g/ (no spirantisation) per michnowicz2012 and lope_blanch1987; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.

sentence: Vamos a comer papadzules y salbutes en el mercado.
ipa: ˈbamos ˈa koˈmeɾ papaˈdsules ˈi saˈlbutes ˈem ˈel meˈɾkado
gloss_en: Let's eat papadzules and salbutes at the market.
notes: Yucatec Maya-origin dishes 'papadzul', 'salbut'; coda /s/ conserved as in the peninsula; occlusive intervocalic /b d g/ (no spirantisation) per michnowicz2012 and lope_blanch1987; word-final /n/->[m] bilabialisation per michnowicz2008 and lope_blanch1987; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.

sentence: Mare, qué caro está todo en la tienda.
ipa: ˈmaɾe ˈke ˈkaɾo eˈsta ˈtodo ˈem ˈla ˈtjenda
gloss_en: Goodness, how expensive everything is at the store.
notes: Yucatec interjection 'mare'; seseo; palatal nasal absent, coverage of peninsular baseline; occlusive intervocalic /b d g/ (no spirantisation) per michnowicz2012 and lope_blanch1987; word-final /n/->[m] bilabialisation per michnowicz2008 and lope_blanch1987; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.

sentence: El pib se cocina bajo la tierra con leña.
ipa: ˈel ˈpib ˈse koˈsina ˈbaho ˈla ˈtjera ˈkom ˈleɲa
gloss_en: The pib is cooked underground with firewood.
notes: Maya 'pib' (earth oven); palatal nasal in 'leña'; seseo baseline; word-final /n/->[m] bilabialisation per michnowicz2008 and lope_blanch1987; /x/->[h] glottal jota per lipski1994 and lope_blanch1987; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.

sentence: La niña anda bien papujo por la gripa.
ipa: ˈla ˈniɲa ˈanda ˈbjem paˈpuho ˈpoɾ ˈla ˈɡɾipa
gloss_en: The little girl looks all puffy from the cold.
notes: Yucatec 'papujo' (puffy-faced); palatal nasal in 'niña'; yeísmo baseline; word-final /n/->[m] bilabialisation per michnowicz2008 and lope_blanch1987; /x/->[h] glottal jota per lipski1994 and lope_blanch1987; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.

sentence: ¿A dónde vas, muchacha, tan de prisa?
ipa: ˈa ˈdonde ˈbas muˈtʃatʃa ˈtam ˈde ˈpɾisa
gloss_en: Where are you going, girl, in such a hurry?
notes: Palatal [tʃ] in 'muchacha'; phrase-final /n/→[m] adstrate not yet modelled (lead); word-final /n/->[m] bilabialisation per michnowicz2008 and lope_blanch1987; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.

sentence: Tomé un pozol bien frío después del trabajo.
ipa: toˈme ˈum poˈsol ˈbjem ˈfɾio deˈspues ˈdel tɾaˈbaho
gloss_en: I had a nice cold pozol after work.
notes: Maya-origin 'pozol' (maize drink); seseo; coda /s/ retained in the peninsula; occlusive intervocalic /b d g/ (no spirantisation) per michnowicz2012 and lope_blanch1987; word-final /n/->[m] bilabialisation per michnowicz2008 and lope_blanch1987; /x/->[h] glottal jota per lipski1994 and lope_blanch1987; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.

sentence: El wixo se puso a llorar cuando cayó la lluvia.
ipa: ˈel ˈwikso ˈse ˈpuso ˈa ʝoˈɾaɾ ˈkwando kaˈʝo ˈla ˈʝubja
gloss_en: The little kid started crying when the rain fell.
notes: Yucatec Maya-flavoured register; yeísmo in 'llorar/lluvia'→[ʝ]; diphthong ⟨ue⟩; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.

sentence: La señora vende panuchos afuera de la iglesia.
ipa: ˈla seˈɲoɾa ˈbende paˈnutʃos aˈfweɾa ˈde ˈla iˈɡlesja
gloss_en: The lady sells panuchos outside the church.
notes: Yucatec 'panucho'; palatal nasal in 'señora'; seseo in 'iglesia'; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.

sentence: No seas malo y compárteme un chan pedazo.
ipa: ˈno ˈseas ˈmalo ˈi koˈmpaɾteme ˈum ˈtʃam peˈdaso
gloss_en: Don't be mean and share a little bit with me.
notes: Maya 'chan' (little) as pre-nominal diminutive; seseo in 'seas'; occlusive intervocalic /b d g/ (no spirantisation) per michnowicz2012 and lope_blanch1987; word-final /n/->[m] bilabialisation per michnowicz2008 and lope_blanch1987; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.

sentence: Anda a traer agua del pozo, chan niña.
ipa: ˈanda ˈa ˈtɾaeɾ ˈaɡa ˈdel ˈposo ˈtʃam ˈniɲa
gloss_en: Go fetch water from the well, little girl.
notes: 'chan' diminutive with 'niña'; palatal nasal in 'niña'; imperative shape; word-final /n/->[m] bilabialisation per michnowicz2008 and lope_blanch1987; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.

sentence: El calor de la península te deja bien cansado.
ipa: ˈel kaˈloɾ ˈde ˈla peˈninsula ˈte ˈdeha ˈbjem kaˈnsado
gloss_en: The peninsula heat leaves you really tired.
notes: Peninsular Yucatec baseline; seseo in 'península'; coverage of retained coda /s/; occlusive intervocalic /b d g/ (no spirantisation) per michnowicz2012 and lope_blanch1987; word-final /n/->[m] bilabialisation per michnowicz2008 and lope_blanch1987; /x/->[h] glottal jota per lipski1994 and lope_blanch1987; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.

sentence: Chan ratito y ya nos vamos para la casa.
ipa: ˈtʃam raˈtito ˈi ˈʝa ˈnos ˈbamos ˈpaɾa ˈla ˈkasa
gloss_en: Just a little while and we'll head home.
notes: 'chan ratito' (Maya-Spanish diminutive stacking); seseo, coda /s/ retained; word-final /n/->[m] bilabialisation per michnowicz2008 and lope_blanch1987; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.

sentence: El niño se cayó de la hamaca en el solar.
ipa: ˈel ˈniɲo ˈse kaˈʝo ˈde ˈla aˈmaka ˈem ˈel soˈlaɾ
gloss_en: The boy fell out of the hammock in the yard.
notes: Yucatec 'solar' (house yard); palatal nasal in 'niño'; yeísmo in 'cayó'→[ʝ]; word-final /n/->[m] bilabialisation per michnowicz2008 and lope_blanch1987; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.

sentence: Compra unos xpelones en el mercado, chan niño.
ipa: ˈkompɾa ˈunos speˈlones ˈem ˈel meˈɾkado ˈtʃam ˈniɲo
gloss_en: Buy some xpelón beans at the market, little boy.
notes: Maya-origin 'xpelón' (bean); 'chan' diminutive; palatal nasal in 'niño'; occlusive intervocalic /b d g/ (no spirantisation) per michnowicz2012 and lope_blanch1987; word-final /n/->[m] bilabialisation per michnowicz2008 and lope_blanch1987; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.

sentence: La muchacha teje una hamaca bien apretada.
ipa: ˈla muˈtʃatʃa ˈtehe ˈuna aˈmaka ˈbjem apɾeˈtada
gloss_en: The girl weaves a really tight hammock.
notes: Yucatec hammock-weaving lexicon; palatal [tʃ] in 'muchacha'; seseo baseline; occlusive intervocalic /b d g/ (no spirantisation) per michnowicz2012 and lope_blanch1987; word-final /n/->[m] bilabialisation per michnowicz2008 and lope_blanch1987; /x/->[h] glottal jota per lipski1994 and lope_blanch1987; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.

sentence: ¿Ya comiste, o qué, chan hermano?
ipa: ˈʝa koˈmiste ˈo ˈke ˈtʃam eˈɾmano
gloss_en: Have you eaten yet or what, little brother?
notes: 'chan' diminutive with kin term; seseo in 'comiste'; phrase-final /n/→[m] not modelled (lead); word-final /n/->[m] bilabialisation per michnowicz2008 and lope_blanch1987; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.

sentence: El abuelo cuenta historias de los aluxes por la noche.
ipa: ˈel aˈbwelo ˈkwenta iˈstoɾjas ˈde ˈlos aˈlukses ˈpoɾ ˈla ˈnotʃe
gloss_en: Grandpa tells stories about the aluxes at night.
notes: Maya mythological 'alux' (sprite); diphthong ⟨ue⟩→[we] in 'cuenta/abuelo'; seseo; occlusive intervocalic /b d g/ (no spirantisation) per michnowicz2012 and lope_blanch1987; seseo/yeismo and retained coda /s/ baseline per lipski1994.
